data.title: Applied Digital most shorted stock in December, NTT DATA sees least interest
data.description:
The S&P 500 technology sector (XLK) advanced 25.24% in the past year, far outpacing the broader markets, which rose 16.72%. In December, XLK moved 0.56% in the upward direction.

Within the tech sector (excluding application software firms), Applied Digital (APLD) was the most shorted stock in December, while NTT DATA (NTDTY) was the least shorted stock.

Stocks with the largest and least short positions

Ranked by short interest as a percentage of shares out

 

 

Applied Digital (APLD), BigBear.ai Holdings (BBAI) and Enphase Energy (ENPH) were the three most shorted stocks in December with short interest of 27.27%, 24.61% and 24.27%, respectively.

Following them were Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS) with a short interest of 22.43% and IonQ (IONQ) at 22.07%.

Microsoft (MSFT) was the second least shorted stock after NTT DATA Group (NTDTY) with an SI of 0.00%. Short interest in the tech giant was 0.71%. Apple (AAPL) was also among the least shorted stocks with an SI of 0.77%.

data.title: Intel narrative 'carrying the day,' but fundamentals are improving: UBS
data.description:
The positive narrative surrounding Intel (INTC) has been “carrying the day,” but the semiconductor company's fundamentals may be starting to improve as well, UBS said. 

“Despite supply that remains tight, we still see an upside bias for FQ4 results on PC [and] server demand strength and see Street numbers (which are about in-line w/normal seasonal) as okay for FQ1 (March) guidance — especially off the stronger Dec Q base,” UBS analyst Timothy Arcuri wrote in a note to clients. “Fundamentally, we see a mixed (albeit improving) picture this year, as CQ1 should also be the worst of INTC's capacity issues (mix shift off Intel 10/7 to more volume for Granite Rapids (Intel 3) and ramp of Panther Lake (18A)).”

Despite that, Arcuri said that memory price increases could halt some of the recent momentum in PC sales. “If INTC guides 2026 revenue (unlikely), we would see low single digits as a reasonable (if not conservative) bar but expect some broader commentary on gross margin (drop-through 40-60%, UBS [estimate] full year ~38%), gross/net capex (UBS [estimate] ~$19B/~$12B or both up slightly Y/Y), and opex $16B (in-line w/ prior guide),” Arcuri explained.

Arcuri has a Neutral rating on Intel, but upped his price target to $49 from $40, on the improving fundamentals. He pointed to the belief that there may be “several” potential external customers — Nvidia (NVDA) (for gaming), Apple (AAPL) (for its M-Series chips), Google (GOOG) (GOOGL), and Broadcom (AVGO) — that may have engaged with Intel on its 14A manufacturing process. 

“Momentum on the 14A narrative should start to build once the PDK for the engineering version of the process is out (we think this Q) and PDK 1.0 is released more toward year-end (this would enable customer designs by 1H:28, now only ~2yrs out),” Arcuri added.

Additionally, Intel may see continued strength in its data center and PC businesses, as the company reiterated a “strong demand environment” for both at UBS' investment conference in December. 

“Given the limited supply on INTC 7/10 nodes, the company is de-emphasizing lower-end PC products which we think will offer near-term tailwinds for the Client business,” Arcuri explained. “While we remain bullish on Data Center demand (UBS forecasts traditional servers growing 6% [year-over-year]), we believe PC demand may moderate from increasing memory pricing given memory accounts for 25-30% of PC BOM. UBS recently revised PC growth forecasts for C2025E/2026E to 7%/-4% from prior 4%/3% and another 2% growth [year-over-year] in C2027E. Total PC units are expected to decline 8% and 8% in [the December quarter and March quarter], but we expect INTC revenues to outperform PC units in [the December quarter] as it benefits from a richer mix.”

Intel will report fourth-quarter results on Jan. 22. A consensus of analysts expect the company to earn $0.08 per share on $13.37B in revenue.

data.title: Central Garden and Honest Company fall after JPMorgan warns on the earnings setup
data.description:
JPMorgan thinks the setup for household personal care stocks into the earnings season is mixed, with the most plain vanilla companies, such as home care and personal care, and with the most exposure to the U.S. consumer, likely to print lackluster top-line results.

The firm downgraded Central Garden & Pet (CENTA) to an Underweight rating from Neutral on its view that the pet category remains subdued. Analyst Andrea Teixeira also noted that volatility in the garden segment remains high and visibility is limited. The expectation is for low single-digit sales and EPS growth.

Meanwhile, The Honest Company (HNST) was hit by a double downgrade from JPMorgan straight to an Underweight rating from Overweight. The negative  stance on the stock centered on the heightened competitive pressure on diapers that is expected to require increased price promotions and price pack architecture for typically higher prices as demanded for clean and natural products. It was also noted that Honest Company's (HNST) new productivity program includes exiting lower-margin, non-strategic categories/channels, which now needs to be replaced by existing revenue sources.

Shares of  Central Garden & Pet Company (CENTA) were down 3.3% in early Friday afternoon trading, while Honest Company (HNST) was down 3.1%.

data.title: J.P. Morgan turns bullish on AutoNation for its brand strategy, long-term prospects
data.description:
• J.P. Morgan upgraded AutoNation (AN) to “overweight” from a previous investment rating of “neutral” on Friday. The research firm praised the company's strategy to build an integrated, brand-centric approach to auto retail via a cohesive experience that spreads across multiple automotive segments.
  • JPM believes that in the longer term, building the AutoNation brand should establish an improved trade-in pipeline, improve customer retention, and likely deliver higher-margin branded F&I and parts & service sales outside the traditional physical vehicle transaction.
  • "The company has shown significant productivity improvements since the pandemic, similar to peers, which should support higher normalized margins as new/used GPUs normalize," JPM said in its January 16 research note.
  • "Relative to peers, we see the balance sheet position as attractive, in addition to brand (less exposed to higher tariff brands) and regional exposure (US only)," they added.
  • AN has an unchanged price target of $235, implying an upside of 9.6%. Shares of the company are up 1.1% before midday.

data.title: Salesforce teams with Mr. Beast on Super Bowl ad
data.description:
• Salesforce (CRM) is teaming with content creator Mr. Beast to create a Super Bowl ad, the enterprise software company said on Friday.
  • The company is not releasing the ad just yet, but shared a teaser video with Seeking Alpha. The teaser shows Mr. Beast (legal name James Stephen Donaldson) entering Salesforce's San Francisco headquarters to visit the company's marketing team to discuss the ad. Donaldson revealed he is in charge of the Marc Benioff-led firm's Super Bowl commercial, which will focus on the company's products and services. “And all I'm gonna say, is this Super Bowl, if you see the commercial, you might become a millionaire,” Mr. Beast said in the tease. “This is epic!”

data.title: M&T Bank outlines 2026 NII target of $7.2B–$7.35B with broad-based loan growth focus
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: M&T Bank Corporation (MTB) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Daryl Bible, Senior EVP & CFO, highlighted the company's expansion in key markets and product innovation, including "opening our new full-service Honey Locust branch" and launching "our new Banking Made for Business suite of business banking solutions tailored to support small and midsized businesses throughout the growth of their life cycle." He emphasized the company's "record net income of $2.85 billion and record EPS of $17," citing "our top quartile return on tangible assets of over 1.4%." The company also "increased our quarterly dividend by 11%, repurchased 9% of our outstanding shares and grew tangible book value per share by 7%."
  • Bible noted significant improvements in asset quality, stating, "nonaccruals decreasing 26% and the nonaccrual percentage of total loans reaching 90 basis points, the lowest since 2007."
  • Bible introduced two enterprise-wide priorities for 2026: "operational excellence" to drive efficiency through automation and process simplification, and "teaming for growth," a coordinated approach across markets and business lines.
  • Bible: "We earned record net income of $2.85 billion and record EPS of $17, while also maintaining our top quartile return on tangible assets of over 1.4%."

Outlook

  • M&T expects taxable equivalent net interest income to be "$7.2 billion to $7.35 billion as net interest margin in the low 3.70s." The outlook assumes "50 basis points of rate cuts in 2026" and includes loan growth in all four main portfolios, with commercial real estate (CRE) balances expected to be lower than 2025's average.
  • Average loans for 2026 are projected at "$140 billion to $142 billion," and average deposits at "$165 billion to $167 billion."
  • Noninterest income is forecast at "$2.675 billion to $2.775 billion" with "growth to be broad-based across our fee income categories and business lines."
  • Total noninterest expense is projected at "$5.5 billion to $5.6 billion," with a first-quarter seasonal salary and benefit increase of $110 million.
  • Full year charge-offs are expected to be "near 40 basis points." CET1 ratio guidance for 2026 is "10.25% to 10.5%."

Financial Results

  • Diluted GAAP EPS for Q4 was $4.67, compared to $4.82 in the prior quarter. Net income was $759 million, down from $792 million in Q3.
  • Net interest income rose to $1.79 billion, up $17 million from the previous quarter. Net interest margin increased to 3.69%.
  • Average loans and leases increased $1.1 billion to $137.6 billion. Average investment securities rose to $36.7 billion, and investment securities and cash held at the Fed totaled $53.7 billion.
  • Noninterest income was $696 million, a decrease from $752 million in Q3. Mortgage banking revenues grew to $155 million, with commercial mortgage banking rising $11 million to $50 million.
  • Noninterest expenses were $1.38 billion, up $16 million from Q3. Salary and benefits declined, while professional services costs increased.
  • Net charge-offs totaled $185 million, or 54 basis points, up from 42 basis points in Q3. Provision for credit losses was $125 million. Allowance for loan losses as a percent of total loans decreased 5 basis points to 1.53%.
  • CET1 ratio was estimated at 10.84%, a decline of 15 basis points from Q3, reflecting $507 million in share repurchases.

Q&A

  • Gerard Cassidy, RBC Capital Markets: Asked about CET1 ratio flexibility and regulatory changes. Bible responded, "We can go a lot lower with where we are today, and we may actually improve that... could we go below 10% at some point? Possibly."
  • Cassidy: Inquired about loan growth drivers, especially in CRE. Bible said, "Our production levels were the strongest they've been for a very long time... we will have growth, as we said, starting in the second quarter on an average basis."
  • Robert Siefers, Piper Sandler: Questioned non-CRE loan growth and M&A strategy. Bible explained, "We're really focused on growing our regional regions this year... We are not aware of anybody who wants to sell in those markets."
  • Matthew O'Connor, Deutsche Bank: Asked about deposit environment. Bible emphasized focus on "growing net new checking accounts, which is really important."
  • Manan Gosalia, Morgan Stanley: Asked about fee and expense growth. Bible said, "We should be about 4% in fee growth is kind of what we're looking there... we are generating positive operating leverage in '26, probably 150 basis points, plus or minus."
  • John Pancari, Evercore: Raised questions on C&I growth and credit quality. Bible described "good overall broad-based growth in all portfolios" and explained consumer delinquencies were due to "more Ginnie Mae repurchases going on the balance sheet."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on capital deployment, loan growth, deposit mix, and credit quality, maintaining a neutral to positive tone. Questions centered on sustainability of growth and risk management.
  • Management was confident and detailed in responses, using phrases like "we feel really good," "we are performing at a very high level," and "I feel really good about that."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management maintained a consistent, constructive tone, with slightly increased management confidence, especially regarding growth outlook and operational improvements.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 net interest income increased to a range of $7.2 billion to $7.35 billion, up from the prior full year outlook of $7 billion to $7.15 billion.
  • CRE loan portfolio is now expected to return to growth point-to-point in 2026, compared to a moderating decline previously.
  • Efficiency ratio improved from 56.9% to 56% for full year 2025, and further to 55.1% in Q4.
  • Management's tone grew more optimistic on fee income growth and operating leverage, while analysts continued to focus on capital flexibility and credit quality.
  • Deposit and loan growth targets were raised, with a renewed emphasis on relationship banking and regional market expansion.
  • Management discussed a shift from siloed operations to a unified "teaming for growth" strategy for 2026.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited possible macroeconomic risks, including a potential slowdown due to a "weakening labor market."
  • Net charge-offs increased, with resolution of previously identified credits impacting Q4 results.
  • Analysts inquired about higher 90-day past dues, which Bible attributed to "more Ginnie Mae repurchases" and "administrative delays."
  • Possible variabilities in net interest income were acknowledged if the "shape of the curve" changes more than anticipated.

Final Takeaway

M&T Bank's leadership signaled strong momentum into 2026, underpinned by record 2025 financials, improved asset quality, and a strategic pivot toward operational excellence and unified growth. The company projects broad-based loan and deposit growth, positive operating leverage, and an expanded net interest income outlook, while remaining disciplined in capital management and focused on generating shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Trump says he is “getting rid” of healthcare middlemen
data.description:
President Donald Trump said on Friday that he will be "getting rid of insurance brokers and corporate middlemen” to reduce healthcare premiums for Americans as part of his “Great Healthcare Plan” unveiled yesterday.

“To further reduce the insurance premiums, my plan ends the giant kickbacks to insurance brokers and corporate middlemen that you’ve been hearing about for so long,” Trump said during a White House event on rural health. “We are getting rid of middlemen,” Trump added.

“It also funds the so-called cost-sharing reduction program to bring down the cost of most common plans on the Exchanges by more than 15%,” he said during the rural health roundtable, which was attended by HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. and other top healthcare officials.

Trump’s remarks came as Americans buying insurance on Obamacare exchanges were slapped with a sharp rise in healthcare premiums for 2026 after Congress failed to extend the COVID-era tax subsidies, which expired on Dec. 31.

The healthcare industry has come under scrutiny lately over soaring drug prices, with lawmakers on both sides blaming the pharmacy benefit managers, which function as middlemen between health insurers, pharmaceutical manufacturers, and pharmacies.

Leading PBMs are run by health insurers such as CVS Health (CVS), Cigna (CI), UnitedHealth (UNH), and Humana (HUM).

Previously, Trump announced plans to redirect subsidy payments directly to individuals, bypassing insurers. “I want to send that extra money straight to health savings accounts ((HSA)) for you, and you can choose your own healthcare,” he added.

Elevance Health (ELV), UnitedHealth Group (UNH), CVS Health (CVS), Centene (CNC), Molina Healthcare (MOH), and Oscar Health (OSCR) run large ACA marketplace businesses. Health Equity (HQY) is a notable HSA provider.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Fed's Bowman sees more risks on employment than inflation
data.description:
Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman said on Friday she is more concerned about the fragility of the labor market than about inflation resurging.

She estimates that, excluding the effect of tariffs, core PCE inflation is close to the Fed's target of 2%. "The underlying trend in core PCE inflation appears to be moving much closer to our 2% target than is currently showing in the data," she said in a speech. "Core services inflation is already roughly consistent with our target, and only core goods inflation remains elevated, but I expect it to start moving down in coming months as the effects of earlier price increases and one-time tariff-related adjustments fade."

Furthermore, less restrictive regulations, lower business taxes, and a more favorable business environment are likely to boost supply through higher productivity, offsetting any negative effects on economic activity and inflation from tariffs, she said.

However, Bowman continues to see downside risks to the Fed's full-employment mandate. "Continued softness in hiring, combined with already low hiring rates, means that even a small decline in demand could translate into a larger increase in unemployment," she said. "Once firms begin to shift from slowing hiring to reducing headcount, labor market conditions could quickly deteriorate."

She judges that current Fed policy is "moderately restrictive."

"Absent a clear and sustained improvement in labor market conditions, we should remain ready to adjust policy to bring it closer to "neutral"—the point at which its interest rate neither stimulates nor drags on economic activity, Bowman said.

"As the economy continues to evolve, policy must evolve with it. My focus will remain on acting early enough to preserve both price stability and a strong labor market," she concluded.

data.title: First Quantum says Panama to authorize ore removal at Cobre copper mine
data.description:
First Quantum Minerals (FQVLF) said Thursday that Panama's government will authorize the removal and processing of stockpiled ore at its shuttered Cobre Panama copper mine.

First Quantum (FQVLF) CEO Tristan Pascall said the move marks a positive step but does not constitute a reopening of the mine, and said the company is awaiting formal approval to carry out the activities in coordination with the government.

The company said the processing of ore stockpiles at the mine will mitigate environmental and operational risks associated with acid rock drainage and ensure a supply of feed material to the tailings management facility.

Panama's President said he expects to decide in the coming months whether to restart the mine, pending the outcome of an audit assessing the condition of the facilities and environmental risks.

The Cobre Panama mine, one of the world's largest open-pit copper deposits, was closed in 2023 following protests from local residents over tax contributions and environmental impacts, and its closure has hurt Panama's economy and First Quantum's (FQVLF) financial prospects.

data.title: U.S. stocks seesaw, even as some chipmakers advance
data.description:
Wall Street on Friday searched for direction, with losses in materials and utilities stocks offset by gains in some chipmakers and the industrial sector. The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) and the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) struggled for momentum.

The Nasdaq (COMP:IND) was last -0.08%, while the S&P (SP500) edged down -0.06%. The blue-chip Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) fell -0.15%. 

Chipmakers extended their gains from the previous session in which they were buoyed by strong results from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) and bargain hunting. Micron (MU) +6.17%, Lam Research (LRCX) +2.38%, and AMD (AMD) +2.23% were among the top percentage gainers on the Nasdaq (COMP:IND).

Over in the bond market, U.S. Treasury yields moved higher across the curve. The shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose to 3.60%, while the benchmark U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) climbed to 4.21%. The longer-end U.S. 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) increased to 4.82%.

Top S&P 500 (SP500) gainers included Super Micro Computer (SMCI) +7.48% and GE Vernova (GEV) +7.20%. Decliners included Constellation Energy (CEG) -9.71%, Vistra (VST) -6.69%, and Albemarle (ALB) -5.10%.
